title: Christella berroi
intro: Christella berroi is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae, commonly known as the Maidenhair fern. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Christella berroi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Christella berroi is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in the form of infusions and salves.
